_disclaimer: DERIVED / UNOFFICIAL. A neutral synthesis of the commencement schedule taken from the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Amendment Act 2024 (Cth) section 2 commencement table, cross-referenced to captured point-in-time compilations. It records what the law provides and when; it takes no position and is not legal or compliance advice. Verify against the official instruments.
title: AML/CTF reform — commencement & point-in-time timeline

events.label: 28th day after assent
events.detail: Examination and information-gathering powers and definitions (Sch 9); REPEAL of the Financial Transaction Reports Act 1988 (Sch 11); transitional provisions enabling the Transitional Rules 2026 (Sch 12).

events.label: Fixed date — main reform tranche
events.detail: AML/CTF programs and business/reporting groups (Sch 1); customer due diligence reform (Sch 2); regulating additional high-risk services — real estate, dealers in precious metals & stones, and professional services, i.e. the Tranche 2 sectors, with enrolment opening (Sch 3); tipping-off offence and disclosure of AUSTRAC information to foreign countries/agencies (Sch 5); services relating to virtual assets (Sch 6 Pt 1); transfers of value and international value transfer services (Sch 8); exemptions (Sch 10).

events.label: Fixed date — Tranche 2 obligations
events.detail: Legal professional privilege (Sch 4); definition of bearer negotiable instrument (Sch 7). Tranche 2 obligations for the newly-covered sectors take effect (the Transitional Rules 2026 frame designated-service timing by reference to the start of 1 July 2026).

states.ref: as made (No. 110, 2024) — source of this schedule

gaps: 1 July 2026 compilation of the AML/CTF Act 2006 — not yet published on the Federal Register as at 2026-07-09; the weekly source monitor will flag it when it appears.
